messages.author: Yellowcooln
messages.text: I wanted to share something I’ve been working on with the Greater Boston Mesh. I spun up an MQTT server using michaelhart/meshcore-mqtt-broker , and vibe coded a live packet tracker site that reads the MQTT feed using michaelhart/meshcore-decoder . Repo: https://github.com/yellowcooln/meshcore-mqtt-live-map/ Live: https://live.bostonme.sh/ I've also built a MQTT Dashboard to help troubleshoot. Still a WIP. https://github.com/yellowcooln/meshcore-mqtt-dashboard # Please @ Me if you need any help! (edited)
messages.content_html: I wanted to share something I’ve been working on with the Greater Boston Mesh. I spun up an MQTT server using michaelhart/meshcore-mqtt-broker, and vibe coded a live packet tracker site that reads the MQTT feed using michaelhart/meshcore-decoder. Repo: https://github.com/yellowcooln/meshcore-mqtt-live-map/ Live: https://live.bostonme.sh/ I've also built a MQTT Dashboard to help troubleshoot. Still a WIP. https://github.com/yellowcooln/meshcore-mqtt-dashboard # Please @ Me if you need any help! (edited)
embeds_html:
GitHub - yellowcooln/meshcore-mqtt-live-map: Live Map of Meshcore M...
Live Map of Meshcore MQTT feed. Contribute to yellowcooln/meshcore-mqtt-live-map development by creating an account on GitHub.

Greater Boston Mesh Map
Live view of Greater Boston Mesh MeshCore nodes, message routes, and advert paths.

GitHub - yellowcooln/meshcore-mqtt-dashboard: A lightweight dashboa...
A lightweight dashboard for MQTT brokers that tracks nodes - yellowcooln/meshcore-mqtt-dashboard

Yellowcooln
I wanted to share something I’ve been working on with the Greater Boston Mesh. I spun up an MQTT server using michaelhart/meshcore-mqtt-broker, and vibe coded a live packet tracker site that reads the MQTT feed using michaelhart/meshcore-decoder. Repo: https://github.com/yellowcooln/meshcore-mqtt-live-map/ Live: https://live.bostonme.sh/ I've also built a MQTT Dashboard to help troubleshoot. Still a WIP. https://github.com/yellowcooln/meshcore-mqtt-dashboard # Please @ Me if you need any help! (edited)

messages.text: in your software, are you using the patched decoder? https://www.npmjs.com/package/meshcore-decoder-multibyte-patch
messages.content_html: in your software, are you using the patched decoder? https://www.npmjs.com/package/meshcore-decoder-multibyte-patch
messages.reply_html:
Erwin (ON8AR)
@Yellowcooln taking the same info, from the MQTT feed from several observers, I was able to create a page with the detailled info of each entry (as far as I know the protocol), including the path. and for the Belgium repeaters, the info is stored, so you can search for a node, by typing in the 1st or more bytes, and by hovering over the path. Also every observer is able to look at his own data only by selecting his observer from the dropdown : https://meshcore.on8ar.eu/ This site is running on my RPi locally for the moment

messages.author: Yellowcooln
messages.text: Please let me know if you have any issues. I would love to actually get them ironed out. I think I did a decent amount of preemptive work. I changed a couple of repeaters around me to send in two byte for the map to learn. So I think it should work. But I don't know it scale
messages.content_html: Please let me know if you have any issues. I would love to actually get them ironed out. I think I did a decent amount of preemptive work. I changed a couple of repeaters around me to send in two byte for the map to learn. So I think it should work. But I don't know it scale

messages.author: Yellowcooln
messages.text: make sure to have the route env's set with what you want to show on the map. This is what boston is running for settings. DEVICE_TTL_HOURS=96 PATH_TTL_SECONDS=172800 TRAIL_LEN=0 HEAT_TTL_SECONDS=600 ROUTE_TTL_SECONDS=120 ROUTE_PAYLOAD_TYPES=8,9,2,5,4 ROUTE_PATH_MAX_LEN=16 ROUTE_MAX_HOP_DISTANCE=100 ROUTE_INFRA_ONLY=true MESSAGE_ORIGIN_TTL_SECONDS=300 ROUTE_HISTORY_ENABLED=true ROUTE_HISTORY_HOURS=24 ROUTE_HISTORY_MAX_SEGMENTS=40000 ROUTE_HISTORY_COMPACT_INTERVAL=120 ROUTE_HISTORY_FILE=/data/route_history.jsonl ROUTE_HISTORY_PAYLOAD_TYPES=8,9,2,5,4 ROUTE_HISTORY_ALLOWED_MODES=path

messages.author: KpaBap
messages.text: Big big major release day today: https://github.com/Kpa-clawbot/meshcore-analyzer/releases/tag/v3.0.0 To upgrade: git pull ./manage.sh stop ./manage.sh setup -> rebuild when prompted v3.0.0 — The Go Rewrite MeshCore Analyzer is now powered by Go. The entire backend — MQTT ingestion, packet decoding, API server, WebSocket broadcast — has been rewritten from Node.js to Go. Same features, same UI, same database. Dramatically faster. This is the biggest change in the project's history. Over 200 commits, 58 issues closed, and a ground-up reimplementation that delivers real, measurable performance gains on every endpoint. ⚡ Performance These are real numbers from production with 56K+ packets: Endpoint Node.js Go Packet queries 30-100ms sub-millisecond (in-memory store) GroupByHash 437ms (9s before store) 97ms Analytics (RF, topology, distance) 1-8 seconds all under 100ms Node health calculation 13 seconds instant (precomputed) Server startup (56K packets) ~9 seconds < 1 second Memory (56K packets) ~1.3 GB ~300 MB The Go server loads all packets into an in-memory store at startup and serves queries directly from RAM. Analytics are precomputed at ingest time — no more scanning the full packet table on every request. TTL caches protect expensive aggregations. The result: every page in the UI feels instant. Thank You This release wouldn't exist without the community: efiten — PR #128 contribution @Jade — testing, feedback, and issue reports throughout the Go migration @crisprCAS9 — issue reports and real-world deployment testing @Uncle Lit — issue reports from production deployments @mibzzer15 — issue reports and edge case discovery And to everyone running MeshCore Analyzer in the wild — your packet data, bug reports, and feature requests are what drive this project forward.
messages.content_html: Big big major release day today: https://github.com/Kpa-clawbot/meshcore-analyzer/releases/tag/v3.0.0 To upgrade: git pull ./manage.sh stop ./manage.sh setup -> rebuild when prompted v3.0.0 — The Go Rewrite MeshCore Analyzer is now powered by Go. The entire backend — MQTT ingestion, packet decoding, API server, WebSocket broadcast — has been rewritten from Node.js to Go. Same features, same UI, same database. Dramatically faster. This is the biggest change in the project's history. Over 200 commits, 58 issues closed, and a ground-up reimplementation that delivers real, measurable performance gains on every endpoint. ⚡ Performance These are real numbers from production with 56K+ packets: Endpoint Node.js Go Packet queries 30-100ms sub-millisecond (in-memory store) GroupByHash 437ms (9s before store) 97ms Analytics (RF, topology, distance) 1-8 seconds all under 100ms Node health calculation 13 seconds instant (precomputed) Server startup (56K packets) ~9 seconds < 1 second Memory (56K packets) ~1.3 GB ~300 MB The Go server loads all packets into an in-memory store at startup and serves queries directly from RAM. Analytics are precomputed at ingest time — no more scanning the full packet table on every request. TTL caches protect expensive aggregations. The result: every page in the UI feels instant. Thank You This release wouldn't exist without the community: efiten — PR #128 contribution @Jade — testing, feedback, and issue reports throughout the Go migration @crisprCAS9 — issue reports and real-world deployment testing @Uncle Lit — issue reports from production deployments @mibzzer15 — issue reports and edge case discovery And to everyone running MeshCore Analyzer in the wild — your packet data, bug reports, and feature requests are what drive this project forward.
